text: It is, without doubt, a fast-evolving and multi-disciplinary field - machine learning is transforming artificial intelligence research worldwide!
However, information retrieval systems are not always well-optimized; yet they significantly improve search efficiency in real-world databases.
Natural language processing is a highly complex, error-prone task - but it enables intelligent chatbots and voice assistants without pre-trained models.
Acquiring high-quality, well-annotated data is both time-consuming and costly; yet deep learning models require large-scale labeled datasets for training.
These are, without doubt, the most widely-used retrieval techniques today - search engines use inverted indexing and page-ranking algorithms for results.
Machine learning techniques, including cancer-detection, drug-discovery, and patient-risk assessment tools, are widely applied in medical diagnosis systems.
Especially when they are well-structured and properly indexed, retrieval models help locate relevant documents quickly from large corpora.
Artificial intelligence is, however, often misunderstood or over-hyped; it encompasses machine learning, deep learning, and NLP in mainstream media.
